title: Romantic Creamy Portuguese Egg Tart
description: A rich, creamy, and romantic Portuguese egg tart featuring a velvety egg wash and a subtle shio-koji cure, making it a low-glycemic-friendly dessert option.

ingredients.name: Low-glycemic-friendly chocolate shavings (e.g., 85% cocoa or higher)

instructions: Roll out the almond flour to a thickness of 1/8 inch (3 mm) between two sheets of parchment paper.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place the dough circles into a muffin tin, pressing them to fit the bottom and sides of each cup.
In a bowl, whisk together the confectioners' sugar, lemon zest, and kosher salt.
Add the eggs, granulated sweetener, and vanilla extract to the bowl, whisking until well combined.
Pour the egg mixture over the dough in each cup, filling them about 3/4 of the way full.
Bake the tarts for 20-22 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the centers are set.
Remove the tarts from the oven and let them cool in the muffin tin for 5 minutes.
Run a knife around the edges of each tart to loosen it, then transfer them to a wire rack to cool completely.
Once the tarts are cool, prepare the shio-koji cure by mixing the shio-koji and water in a small bowl.
Brush the egg wash over the cooled tarts, then sprinkle with the shio-koji cure.
Refrigerate the tarts for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, sprinkle with low-glycemic-friendly chocolate shavings, if desired.
Serve the tarts chilled, garnished with additional shio-koji cure and chocolate shavings if desired.
